As noted in the previous sections, I want to be very much more purposeful with what I post, when I post, how I interact, when I interact. I'm getting a notebook spread together to start making more in depth notes now that the semester has almost wrapped up. I feel like if I get in the habit NOW, over break, it'll be way easier to track when the semester starts back up in late january. Habits are built by continuous effort and pushing forward regardless of what happens IRL!!! We've got this! 

I also think it helps a lot to connect with any new viewers, asking them what sorts of things they like, what their day was like, try to cheer them up if they had a bad day! That really has been a saving grace of a lot of my streams - being a positive influence on those who have a harder time to see positive in their days!

IRIAM Streaming Notes:
Time: 6:01:05
Followers: 12
I had my community send me written works they had written for story time and it was so much fun. At least 6 people submitted and some made us tear up because of how good they were. The highlight I think was when everyone was sharing a little about themselves and wasn't rude or making jokes. They felt as if they can be safe in my community and welcomed. I felt super proud in that moment and they all seemed to have fun.

We spoke about various things during the wind down and they stayed for it. New people came in. However when a 9 year old came into my stream my chat respected me talking to them about how this app is 18+ and encouraged the child to focus on their own education and seek out platforms that are safer along with basic internet safety. My community said I did very well handling the situation. I did report the account and blocked them from my stream. It showed just how respectful those who are in my chat and how we all want a space to have fun but not worry about much else.

I don’t really know how to feel. I know I won’t benefit from a break (streaming makes me happy) but I feel like things keep going wrong when it comes to my luck recently. The algorithm is strange, and it feels like I’m behind my peers. I try and stream daily, put out some good content, and I’ve begun looking at what my peers with more success than I are doing— and I’m trying to replicate that, but it feels like I keep being met with failure.
I’m starting to re evaluate what I want when it comes to my future plans. I want to participate in the sake event and win that! However, there seems to be something wrong with me, my content, or something else— that or my luck is rotten or I’m just being too harsh on myself. It’s just not even cracking 1,000 points in the bun event when everyone else got 3,000 or more makes me wonder if I got too hopeful or overestimated my potential

Reflection / Comments (Optional):
This was a short stream because of my voice going out. My followers knew about it due to a post on x. However, they still came. Around 8-10 people stopped by and average of 6 stayed until the end of my stream. They were ok with talking among themselves. I did speak to them of course but they were understanding it wouldn't be a lot.

I am happy that I am making a very understanding group of viewers. They are talking among themselves as if they are friends and they have gotten along with each other.

I think next I will try to be more comfortable having dead air sometimes so I do not strain my throat out again. That will be my next and biggest challenge is balance of talking vs activity in streams.
